fields.description:
## Why
Automate the FFO rebase-to-tip-then-submit so parallel agent sessions stop hand-rolling it and racing; serialize so the tested tree is always the merged tree with no thrash.

## What
A project service (Python, project infra â€” NOT `src/rebar` core; lives at `infra/autolander/loop.py`) that, for the front `Autosubmit`+submittable change/chain: rebases to the current tip, awaits a fresh `Verified +1`, and ancestor-atomic submits the exact tested SHA. Holds a single in-flight `wipChain`.

**Poll cadence (design-fixed).** The loop runs on a fixed **15 s poll interval** â€” the "bot poll interval" that S4 and S5 reference. Rationale: a freshly-`Autosubmit`'d change is picked up within ~15 s (good agent UX) while staying gentle on the Gerrit REST API; it is intentionally distinct from `land`'s own 30 s client-side poll (S4). The bot writes its liveness heartbeat once per poll iteration (S5 AC5), so under normal operation the heartbeat is never older than ~15 s.

**Terminology â€” `submittable` under FFO.** Under Fast-Forward-Only `main` (ADR-0040), Gerrit's `submittable` field **already requires the change to be a descendant of the current `main` tip**: a behind-tip change is reported **non-submittable** (`submittable=false`), and `submittable=True` means both gate votes are at MAX *and* the change sits on the current tip. Two consequences this loop must respect: (1) **TOCTOU** â€” `submittable=True` is only true as of the read; `main` can advance before `POST /submit` and flip the change to non-submittable, so re-confirm submittability immediately before submitting, and if a stale submit is nonetheless attempted FFO refuses it with a `not fast-forward` error. (2) A native `submittable=false` does **not** say *why* (behind-tip vs a failing gate vote) â€” the distinction S4 needs for `needs_rebase`.

## Dependencies
Depends on **S1** (`sterilized-papery-horse`, 843b-801f-9ef9-442d) â€” the `Autosubmit` label + its **requester-votable ACL** (the vote is granted to group `Contributors`, so an *agent* sets the label under its OWN identity via S4's `land`; this loop casts no votes of its own). The loop only *selects on* `label:Autosubmit+1`, so the label and its Contributor-votable ACL must exist and be live before the loop can select or drive anything.

## Preconditions (declared elsewhere, required live for this loop)
- The bot service account must hold **Submit on `refs/heads/*`** (project.config restricts `submit` to `Contributors` + `Administrators` with `exclusiveGroupPermissions = submit`), or every `POST /submit` returns 403. Granted by S5 AC2 (submit permission on `refs/heads/*`).
- `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` requires the bot to hold the **`rebaseOnBehalfOfUploader`** server-level capability (`All-Projects` `refs/meta/config`) â€” a scoped, minimum-necessary impersonation grant, NOT broad `Impersonate`. Provisioned by S5 AC2.
- The bot's Gerrit HTTP credential is supplied via S5's SSM-backed secret (never inlined) â€” see S5 AC2.

## Scope
The core serial loop: selection + rebase-to-tip + fresh-Verified await + ancestor-atomic submit + serialization + identity/attribution, **including the FFO TOCTOU re-drive** (core-loop resilience against `main` advancing mid-flight). The distinct **failure paths** â€” CI-fail (`Verified -1`) and rebase-conflict (`409`) hand-back â€” plus deploy and the agent-facing command are separate stories (S3, S5, S4). The N=5-exhaustion park reuses S3's `needs_rebase` hand-back mechanism.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Selects the front `label:Autosubmit+1 is:submittable` change/chain; **FIFO ordering keyed on the `Autosubmit` vote's timestamp** â€” read from the `ApprovalInfo.date` field returned under the `DETAILED_LABELS` option (`GET /changes/?q=â€¦&o=DETAILED_LABELS`); oldest-voted-submittable-first. (`is:submittable` is the point-in-time candidate filter; the change is only *landed* after the pre-submit re-confirmation â€” see the TOCTOU guard below.) *(Precondition to verify against the live Gerrit 3.14.1: that `ApprovalInfo.date` is populated for the custom `Autosubmit` label; existing repo code reads only the integer `value`.)*
- [ ] **Rebase-selection rule:** detect a relation chain via `GET /changes/{id}/revisions/current/related` (the RelatedChanges endpoint) â€” a chain of >1 non-merge member rebases with `POST /changes/{id}/rebase:chain` (this endpoint exists in Gerrit 3.6+, so 3.14.1 supports it; it requires a **linear ancestry** â€” single parenting throughout the chain â€” and does not accept `committer_email`). A **`--no-ff` merge change is ALWAYS rebased with `POST /changes/{id}/rebase`** (first-parent-only â†’ `MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE`), **NEVER `rebase:chain`**, even when it sits at a chain tip â€” `rebase:chain` is for linear non-merge chains only. A single non-merge change also uses `POST /changes/{id}/rebase`. The `RebaseInput` field **`rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader=true`** preserves author/DCO `Signed-off-by`/`rebar-ticket` trailer. *(Precondition to probe against the live instance: the `rebase:chain` path and the `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` body key.)*
- [ ] Awaits a **fresh** `Verified +1` â€” a `Verified +1` on the exact rebased patchset SHA (not a copied/carried vote from a prior patchset). **For a multi-member chain, `rebase:chain` mints a NEW patchset (distinct SHA) for EVERY member, so ALL members must carry a fresh `Verified +1` on their respective post-rebase SHAs before submit** â€” submitting while any intermediate member lacks a fresh Verified would land an untested tree (violates the R5 safety guarantee). The await is **bounded** (default 30 min, configurable, matching `land`'s timeout): if no fresh `Verified` arrives (CI hung), treat as a CI failure and hand the stack back rather than blocking forever. (`LLM-Review` carries `TRIVIAL_REBASE`/`MERGE_FIRST_PARENT_UPDATE` per ADR-0041; `Verified` re-runs on each new patchset.)
- [ ] **Ancestor-atomic submit** = a single `POST /changes/{tip-id}/submit` on the chain tip; under FFO with `change.submitWholeTopic`/relation-chain semantics this submits the tip together with all its ancestors atomically (all-or-nothing), never landing a partial stack. **FFO TOCTOU guard + re-drive:** `submittable=True` is only true as of the read â€” `main` can advance before `POST /submit`, and under FFO that flips the change to non-submittable (ADR-0040). So immediately before `POST /submit`, re-confirm the tested tree is still submittable; for a chain the check that matters is that the **bottom-most member's parent is still `main`'s current tip** (the tip change's parent is its predecessor in the chain, not `main`). If it is no longer submittable (main advanced) or any tested SHA changed, do NOT submit. If a stale submit is nonetheless attempted, treat the `not fast-forward` refusal identically. In either case **re-drive the same `wipChain`**: reset its `phase` to `rebasing`, rebase-to-new-tip â†’ await fresh Verified (all members) â†’ re-check, looping **at most `N=5` times** (`re_drive_count` in the schema below); after `N=5` re-drives park the change and surface `needs_rebase` rather than casting a stale submit. *(The concrete park/hand-back mechanism is defined jointly with S3 â€” see that story's `needs_rebase` marker.)*
- [ ] **`wipChain` schema:** a single in-memory record `{change_id, chain_member_ids[], tested_shas{change_idâ†’sha}, verified_at{change_idâ†’ts}, phase, re_drive_count}` held by the single instance, where **`phase âˆˆ {selecting, rebasing, awaiting_verified, submitting, idle, paused}`** (`paused` for S5's emergency-stop); `tested_shas`/`verified_at` are per-member maps (a single-change stack has one entry), set to the post-rebase patchset SHA(s) on entering `awaiting_verified` and cleared on `idle`; `re_drive_count` tracks the bounded TOCTOU re-drive loop (reset on selecting a new front).
- [ ] Single `wipChain` / single instance (serialized) â€” never rebases multiple changes onto the same HEAD concurrently.
- [ ] Reads existing `Verified` votes only; casts NO votes (no gate-semantics change in Phase 1).
- [ ] **Verification/tests:** unit tests cover the state machine (each `phase` transition), FIFO ordering, the merge-vs-chain-vs-single rebase-selection branching, fresh-Verified detection (including the all-members-fresh rule for a chain), and the TOCTOU guard + bounded N=5 re-drive (SHA mismatch / `main` advancing mid-flight). A live/E2E check drives one real `Autosubmit` change through selectâ†’rebaseâ†’await-Verifiedâ†’submit against the Gerrit 3.14.1 instance (proving the `rebase:chain` path and `rebase_on_behalf_of_uploader` key). Recorded as `set_verify_commands` DD entries so done-ness is provable without inventing a harness.
